content: US macroeconomic conditions remain in selective rotation phase. Headline growth at 2.1% real GDP year-over-year with disinflationary impulse continuing as core PCE prints at 2.4%. The labor market exhibits orderly cooling with payrolls running 145k three-month average. Valuation metrics at -1.00 across the S&P 500 versus 10-year average earnings, reflecting elevated multiples compressed by recent rate normalization. Market entropy at 0.50 indicates orderly rotation: breadth holding firm despite mega-cap leadership concentration, VIX at 14 (35th percentile), issuance calendar manageable. Risk-on sentiment dominates the tape with bullish positioning into year-end seasonals, though signs of complacency are emerging. Reflationary undertones persist in commodity-linked names supported by the soft-landing scenario.
